programmes.show_description: The two camps battle it out in an epic trial to decide who will win dinner that evening and who will be on the rice and beans. As Savannah scrub comes to an end, the campmates must take on a petrifying challenge that will push them to their limits. The camps finally come back together in a moment which sees joy and confrontation in equal measures. Ant and Dec host
Daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection

Just 25 players are left as Rob Brydon hosts another tense round of quick-fire quiz duels, with each win expanding a player's territory, while each loss sends another home. With a £5,000 Floor bonus on the line and a £50,000 jackpot up for grabs at the end of the series, it's all to play for as friends turn to foes and challenge one another
Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features
Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more
The key headlines from the day so far
Adil Ray hosts the game of words as Emmerdale actors Lisa Riley and Mark Charnock take on the Rev Richard Coles and comedian Tim Vine. They all try to win big for their chosen charities

programmes.show_name: STV Racing Live: Grand National Festival
programmes.show_description: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of Ladies Day, which is the second day from Aintree, where the feature races are the 3.30 JCB Melling Chase and the 4.05 Randox Topham Chase, and races also take place at 1.45, 2.20, 2.55, and 4.40. With analysis from AP McCoy, Ruby Walsh and Mick Fitzgerald, reports by Matt Chapman, Rishi Persad, Alice Plunkett and Luke Harvey, betting news from Brian Gleeson, Social Stable thoughts with Oli Bell and Sam Quek, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

Bradley Walsh presents Andrea from Wolverhampton, Reece from London, David from Neath and Nesta from Devizes pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programmes.show_name: STV News at Six and Weather
programmes.show_description: Including regional weather forecast
By the Scottish Green Party
A round-up of the day's news headlines and talking points from the UK and around the world
Movie news, chat about favourite TV programmes, and music. Plus, catch-ups with some of the biggest names in worldwide entertainment
Robert decides it is time to come clean, while Dawn is shocked and Kerry apologises
Sarah quizzes Danielle about Theo's violent streak, Bernie hears promising news of Mal's future plans, George meets an estate agent, and Carl gives Summer a clue to the identity of Billy's killer

As contestants near the end of their first week in camp, it remains to be seen who will go the distance and who will be left licking their wounds. In this edition, two campmates must face their ultimate fears as they are literally dropped from a great height. Ant and Dec host

Nicola Sturgeon faces a grilling from a cast of autistic and learning-disabled interviewers. Why did she separate from her husband? Who does she like least in politics? As the questions come thick and fast, Nicola opens up about grief, guilt and the loss of her baby, reflects on her complicated relationship with former mentor Alex Salmond, and reveals what she'd say to her younger self
A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

Lee Mack hosts a special edition of the quiz in which the prize pot that is not won on one show is carried over to the next day's edition, meaning a potential jackpot of £500,000. What's more, anyone who makes it through to the final question automatically comes back to compete again the following day

As contestants near the end of their first week in camp, it remains to be seen who will go the distance and who will be left licking their wounds. In this edition, two campmates must face their ultimate fears as they are literally dropped from a great height. Ant and Dec host
Daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection

Jimmy Doherty and Shivi Ramoutar in the kitchen by musician Tanita Tikaram for cooking, chat and craft. After Japanese salmon miso and natto for breakfast, Jimmy and Shivi set about rustling up a peanut stew before all three try making homemade treats for pets. Shivi gets her waders on for an encounter with beavers at a city rewilding project, Jimmy visits a family doughnut kitchen and store in Florida, and there's a new twist on a classic margarita cocktail
Adil Ray hosts the game of words as journalist Cathy Newman and wordsmith Gyles Brandreth compete against Loose Women's Kaye Adams and Morning Live's Dr Ranj Singh for charity

In Wakefield, Alan and the team create a secluded secret garden filled with hidden corners and magical touches for a wheelchair-using girl and her family, featuring a playful water feature and plant-petting corner, filled with sensory plants. Meanwhile in Swansea, Katie Rushworth creates a modern, sleek, sunken secret garden for a woman who throws parties for the elderly to ease loneliness

programmes.show_name: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programmes.show_description: Joining Alan at Manor Farm for a special Easter Sunday show are actress Sheila Hancock and West End star Julian Ovenden, while florist Simon Lycett has the best in Easter bouquets. Plus, a couple who have set up a hedgehog hospital in their back garden, and things get very chocolatey in the Best of British feature with drinks expert Tom Surgey
A round-up of the latest headlines
Build-up to the Grand National at Aintree
James cooks for Strictly Come Dancing stars Amy Dowden and Carlos Gu, while there are four pasta recipes from Francesco Mazzei, and a tomato masterclass with gardener David Domoney
Round-up of the latest headlines

Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of day three from Aintree, featuring the support races prior to the Grand National, including the 3.05 Liverpool Hurdle, and races at 1.20, 1.55 and 2.30. With analysis from AP McCoy, Ruby Walsh and Mick Fitzgerald, reports by Matt Chapman, Rishi Persad, Alice Plunkett and Luke Harvey, betting news from Brian Gleeson, Social Stable thoughts with Oli Bell and Sam Quek, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of the 4.00 Grand National from Aintree, the most prestigious race on the British calendar. Nick Rockett was ridden to victory by Patrick Mullins last year at a price of 33/1. With analysis from AP McCoy, Ruby Walsh and Mick Fitzgerald, reports by Matt Chapman, Rishi Persad, Alice Plunkett and Luke Harvey, betting news from Brian Gleeson, Social Stable thoughts with Oli Bell and Sam Quek, and commentary from Richard Hoiles
Round-up of the latest headlines

Jeremy Clarkson hosts the exciting and fast-paced quiz where each episode sees six hopefuls compete for £1 million - however, only one player can take their place in the Hot Seat at a time and answer multiple-choice questions to work their way up the Money Ladder

Stephen Mulhern hosts another charity edition of the game show, with Stephen Mangan, Kemi Rodgers and Tom Allen all trying to say what they see, as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen good causes

Ant and Dec host as the auditions continue, with more acts taking to the stage with performances to impress the crowd and judges Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon and KSI. Which acts will win themselves a coveted space in the semi-finals and the chance of a prize of £250,000 and a sought-after slot at the Royal Variety Performance.

Joel Dommett, Judi Love, Sam Thompson and GK Barry deceive unsuspecting members of the public as they take part in SOS: Survive or Surrender, hosted by former commando Jason Fox, who is in on the whole thing. Actor and comedian Guz Khan is the guest saboteur

Quiz game show hosted by Lee Mack in which the questions have nothing to do with remembering facts, but are all about logic and common sense. A hundred contestants begin every show, but who will make it to the end and answer a question only one per cent of the country can get right - and win up to £100,000?
Jonathan is joined by Small Prophets star Michael Palin, and actresses Catherine Tate and Anna Maxwell Martin, while there is music from singer-songwriter Myles Smith
Round-up of the latest headlines

Action from the latest matches, including Coventry City v Sheffield Wednesday at Coventry Building Society Arena, Norwich City v Ipswich Town at Carrow Road and Sheffield United v Hull City at Bramall Lane in the Championship
